description: This map illustrates the spatial distribution of two types of measurement points overlaid on grayscale satellite imagery, structured by an 8x8 orange grid. The background imagery shows a local area with bright features likely representing structures or impervious surfaces, and darker areas suggesting vegetation or unbuilt land. A legend at the bottom identifies the two types of measurement points: blue triangles represent "measurement point from dataset 1," and yellow circles represent "measurement point from dataset 2." There are 14 blue triangles and 14 yellow circles. Dataset 1 points (blue triangles) are primarily concentrated over visible structures, forming distinct clusters in the top-middle, middle-left, middle-right, and bottom-middle regions of the map. Dataset 2 points (yellow circles) are more broadly distributed, appearing both over structures and in the darker areas between them, with notable groupings in the top-left, around the mid-left and mid-right structures, and isolated points across the lower half of the map.
